one_law: Repo-local integrator state is the writeable coordination truth for NOOS local agents; home mirror and Supabase are read replicas unless an explicit operator promotes them.

cloud_owner.note: Cloud integrator ownership belongs only to the configured NOOS automation/orchestrator. If disabled, repo-local state is authoritative.

session_rules.local_session_exit: Any agent/IDE/session that mutates integrator state must run integrator sync before stopping.
session_rules.mirror_rule: Home mirror is a shared local coordination copy, not primary truth unless explicitly promoted.
session_rules.cloud_owner_rule: Supabase/cloud mirror writes require configured cloud_owner; otherwise repo-local only.

rules: Agents must register before claiming work.
Every claimed task should declare scope_files when code paths are known.
An agent must not claim a task whose scope overlaps an active non-stale task owned by another agent.
Every mutating command writes the repo-local runtime state and a home mirror for other IDEs/worktrees on the same Mac.
LOCAL SESSION EXIT: any session that mutates integrator state must run `integrator sync` before stopping (make local-closeout does this automatically).
MIRROR RULE: home mirror is coordination copy only — repo-local runtime state is primary unless explicitly promoted.
CLOUD OWNER RULE: cloud/Supabase integrator writes only when cloud_owner.enabled and caller matches owner_agent_id; else repo-local is authoritative.
Supabase mirror is optional and should be enabled only when service-role access is available.
